title: News and updates | Employment New Zealand
content:
## News and updates Ngā rongo kōrero me ngā whakahou
News and media releases from Employment New Zealand.
- 5 Dec 2025 Former Wendy’s operator loses bid to overturn improvement notice The former owners of the Wendy’s chain of fast-food restaurants in New Zealand have lost a bid in the Employment Relations Authority (ERA) to overturn an improvement notice involving non-payment of employees on public holidays going back 6 years.
The former owners of the Wendy’s chain of fast-food restaurants in New Zealand have lost a bid in the Employment Relations Authority (ERA) to overturn an improvement notice involving non-payment of employees on public holidays going back 6 years.
- 24 Nov 2025 MBIE compliance teams swoop on Coromandel ahead of holiday season Compliance and enforcement teams from the Ministry of Business, Innovation and Employment (MBIE) have made unannounced visits to Coromandel district businesses ahead of the holiday season to check they are meeting minimum employment standards.
Compliance and enforcement teams from the Ministry of Business, Innovation and Employment (MBIE) have made unannounced visits to Coromandel district businesses ahead of the holiday season to check they are meeting minimum employment standards.
- 5 Nov 2025 MBIE launches crackdown on non-compliance in beauty industry Compliance and enforcement teams from the Ministry of Business, Innovation and Employment (MBIE) have launched a targeted enforcement initiative across the central North Island in response to growing concerns about employment practices and non-compliance in the beauty industry. Migrant exploitation
Compliance and enforcement teams from the Ministry of Business, Innovation and Employment (MBIE) have launched a targeted enforcement initiative across the central North Island in response to growing concerns about employment practices and non-compliance in the beauty industry.
- Migrant exploitation
- 7 Oct 2025 Whangārei hospitality businesses in Labour Inspectorate spotlight The Labour Inspectorate has launched an intensive compliance operation in the Whangārei region with 29 businesses visited by Labour Inspectors.
The Labour Inspectorate has launched an intensive compliance operation in the Whangārei region with 29 businesses visited by Labour Inspectors.
- 22 Sept 2025 Are you ready for a visit from the Labour Inspectorate? If you're an employer, it's important to understand what to expect if the Labour Inspectorate visits your workplace – and how to make sure you're prepared.
If you're an employer, it's important to understand what to expect if the Labour Inspectorate visits your workplace – and how to make sure you're prepared.
- 15 Sept 2025 Personal grievances: What employers need to know Unjustified dismissal or unjustified disadvantage are common reasons employees raise personal grievances. Here is a quick guide for employers to help you understand what these terms mean and avoid common mistakes.
Unjustified dismissal or unjustified disadvantage are common reasons employees raise personal grievances. Here is a quick guide for employers to help you understand what these terms mean and avoid common mistakes.
- 8 Sept 2025 New protections for employees discussing pay The Employment Relations (Employee Remuneration Disclosure) Amendment Act 2025 passed into law on 27 August 2025, introducing new legal protections for employees.
The Employment Relations (Employee Remuneration Disclosure) Amendment Act 2025 passed into law on 27 August 2025, introducing new legal protections for employees.
- 5 Sept 2025 Research shows improving knowledge of rights and obligations The 2025 Employment Monitor has been released. Read about the findings from the research.
The 2025 Employment Monitor has been released. Read about the findings from the research.
- 7 Aug 2025 MBIE regulatory teams zero in on Central Otago ski resort towns Teams from the Ministry of Business, Innovation and Employment (MBIE) led by the Labour Inspectorate have launched a sweeping compliance monitoring operation in the Central Otago region.
Teams from the Ministry of Business, Innovation and Employment (MBIE) led by the Labour Inspectorate have launched a sweeping compliance monitoring operation in the Central Otago region.
- 6 Aug 2025 Joint government operation boosts compliance awareness in Ashburton hospitality sector A coordinated government operation by the Labour Inspectorate and Ashburton Alcohol Licencing has brought compliance awareness to the forefront of Ashburton’s hospitality sector.
A coordinated government operation by the Labour Inspectorate and Ashburton Alcohol Licencing has brought compliance awareness to the forefront of Ashburton’s hospitality sector.
